top_sources.top_context: indefinitely. This is a targeted, time-bound intervention when conception matters. Once family planning goals are met, most men discontinue hMG and return to hCG alone or nothing at all. If hCG is the backbone, hMG is the accelerator when you need it. ——————————————————————————— **Kisspeptin** Kisspeptin is often misunderstood. Mechanistically, it sits upstream of everything else. It stimulates GnRH release in the hypothalamus, which then triggers LH and FSH from the pituitary. In theory, that sounds perfect. One signal, full axis activation. top_sources.top_snippet: e inflammation throughout the body. Epitalon, produced in the pineal gland, controls circadian rhythm and telomerase activity. As it declines, sleep quality deteriorates, cellular repair slows, and th
top_sources.top_context: e inflammation throughout the body. Epitalon, produced in the pineal gland, controls circadian rhythm and telomerase activity. As it declines, sleep quality deteriorates, cellular repair slows, and the aging clock accelerates. Even peptides tied to mood and reproduction, like Oxytocin and Kisspeptin, drop significantly. This contributes to the emotional detachment, lower libido, and reduced bonding capacity that so often accompany later life. Each of these declines has a measurable effect on human function and can impact our healthspan. ——————————————————————————— **The Scale of L

taylor-williams.female_specific_note: Taylor Williams Coaching focuses on female-specific HRT/peptide guidance. Per-peptide notes here often emphasize female dosing, fertility, hormone interactions.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider kisspeptin primarily as a libido / sexual-function and HPG-axis-rhythm tool rather than a TRT or fertility cornerstone, since Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 02:00:40.120): "Can I use kisspeptin as a substitute? I mean, you could. It's just not going to really do anything" and Hunter email threadId 19c353335ff91510 (2026-02-06): "In practice, its short half-life makes it difficult to use as a primary fertility agent in men on TRT." We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh dosing route and frequency because Hunter's 2025 protocol uses "125 mcg twice daily" subQ from a 3,333 mcg/mL reconstitution while Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:04:35.320): "this is what I would do personally is 100 micrograms subcutaneously… every evening for 30 days," and we'd flag that endogenous decline is the rationale per Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "Endogenous kisspeptin neurons + receptors decline with age."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Not a TRT replacement: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 02:00:43.640): "I would use HCG before I would use kisspeptin for TRT"; Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "Won't help primary gonadal failure — if testes/ovaries can't respond, kisspeptin won't fix the downstream organ issue. Poor substitute for TRT."
- Not a primary fertility agent in men on TRT: Hunter email threadId 19c353335ff91510 (2026-02-06): "its short half-life makes it difficult to use as a primary fertility agent in men on TRT" and "compared to hCG, hMG, and enclomiphene, it's not my first, second, or third choice for improving sperm parameters."
- Short half-life requires frequent dosing: Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "Half-life is short → smaller, more frequent injections work best"; Hunter email threadId 19c353335ff91510 (2026-02-06): "It's in circulation for minutes, not hours. Sustained effects would likely require multiple daily injections, which isn't practical for most people."
- Female cycle gating: Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:05:13.520): "bleeding and on your period is the only time you're not going to be taking this peptide"; Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:05:06.600): "taking this day 6 through 28th of my menstrual cycle."
- Reconstitution + injection details (Hunter's 2025 protocol): Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "10 mg vial + 3 mL bacteriostatic water," "Concentration: 3,333 mcg/mL," "Gently swirl, don't shake," "0.04 mL = 4 units subQ abdomen."
- Best candidates per Hunter: Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "Men with low T from CENTRAL causes — pituitary under-signalling (low LH/FSH), testes still functional," "Women with irregular cycles or fertility issues," "Low libido/sexual desire problems — when labs look fine but desire low," "Aging men or post-menopausal women — bone density + hormone mitigation."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Both Hunter and Taylor frame kisspeptin as a hormonal-axis / sexual-function and post-menopausal-support tool, dosed subcutaneously into abdominal fat, with cycling. Sexual function: Hunter email threadId 19c353335ff91510 (2026-02-06): "Where kisspeptin consistently shines is in libido. There's solid data showing it activates brain sexual arousal centers and increases penile blood flow"; Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:04:25.840): "if you're using this peptide for more of a sex enhancement and try to help with your sexual health." Bone / aging: Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29) under "Bone Health": "↑Osteocalcin (bone formation marker)" and "Aging men or post-menopausal women — bone density + hormone mitigation"; Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:20:25.480): "kisspeptin is going to help… with the bone density," Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:20:37.720): "it's also going to help with anti-aging," Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:20:41.679): "it's going to help with skin elasticity." Route + injection site: Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "0.04 mL = 4 units subQ abdomen"; Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:04:38.880): "100 micrograms subcutaneously meaning we are going to inject that into our belly fats." Mechanism / HPG axis: Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "Major 'on-switch' for HPG axis" and "Brain hypothalamus releases GnRH when kisspeptin activates it → pituitary releases LH+FSH → ovaries/testes produce sex hormones"; Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:20:21.919): "menopause with losing our progesterone and our estrogen levels kisspeptin is going to help… stabilize those hormones that have decreased."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Standing daily dose differs: Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "125 mcg twice daily (morning + evening, 8-12 hr apart)" — i.e., 250 mcg/day split — versus Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:04:35.320): "100 micrograms subcutaneously… every evening for 30 days" — i.e., 100 mcg/day single evening dose. Female cycle ceiling vs men's micro-pulsing: Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:05:43.600): "you could take up to um 200 micrograms" in luteal phase or for fertility/PCOS, while Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29) keeps the pulsed-twice-daily 125 mcg frame: "Twice-daily micro-pulsing mimics natural kisspeptin rhythms → smoother HPG activation, no receptor fatigue from higher bolus doses." Use-case framing: Hunter email threadId 19c353335ff91510 (2026-02-06) treats kisspeptin as a sexual-function layer rather than a fertility tool — "I see kisspeptin as something you might layer in for sexual function while using more reliable agents for fertility preservation or conception" — while Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:05:54.160): "struggling with… fertility or with um PCOS that's where I would… maybe bump that up" frames kisspeptin as part of the fertility / cycle-restoration protocol in women. No Trevor Bachmeyer kisspeptin content present in this card's expert sources.

chronic.dose: Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "125 mcg twice daily (morning + evening, 8-12 hr apart)"; Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:04:35.320): "100 micrograms subcutaneously… every evening for 30 days"; Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:05:43.600): "you could take up to um 200 micrograms" (women, luteal / fertility / PCOS context)

chronic.frequency: Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "twice daily (morning + evening, 8-12 hr apart)" with "Half-life is short → smaller, more frequent injections work best"; Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:04:43.800): "every evening for 30 days" with cycle gating Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:05:06.600): "day 6 through 28th of my menstrual cycle"
chronic.duration: Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "Cycle" "8-12 weeks"; Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:04:45.120): "every evening for 30 days"

reset_biology_synthesis.female_specific_notes: Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:04:35.320): "this is what I would do personally is 100 micrograms subcutaneously… every evening for 30 days." Cycle gating Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:05:06.600): "taking this day 6 through 28th of my menstrual cycle" with Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:05:13.520): "bleeding and on your period is the only time you're not going to be taking this peptide." Higher ceiling for cycle / fertility / PCOS context Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:05:43.600): "you could take up to um 200 micrograms" and Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:05:53.160): "struggling with… fertility or with um PCOS that's where I would" with luteal-phase bump Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:06:13.560): "go a little bit higher when I'm in my ludal phase of my cycle just to help with the PMS um side effects." Menopausal-support frame Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:20:21.919): "menopause with losing our progesterone and our estrogen levels kisspeptin is going to help… with the bone density… stabilize those hormones that have decreased" with Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:20:37.720): "it's also going to help with anti-aging" and Taylor (A0bKJkAacx0 00:20:41.679): "skin elasticity… hair quality." Hunter's 2025 frame echoes the female angle Hunter email threadId 19a31ff6dc2532d4 (2025-10-29): "Women with irregular cycles or fertility issues — ovulation triggers, HPG rhythm restoration, hypothalamic amenorrhea, IVF" and "Aging men or post-menopausal women — bone density + hormone mitigation."
